suppose the above chain has included a subsidy of $300 that is given to the jam manufacturers

replies.msg: 呢句唔係講佢係at market price咩 所以我加咗

好簡短講下Paper 2啦，如果有錯記得提返我
Q1. 填海的機會成本
1a. 不變
1b. 上升
2a. Positive statement (實證性陳述)
2b. Equalize income (收入均等化)
3. Public corporation的例子可以睇返教科書
4. 疫苗 - Positive externality
5a. Division of labour/Specialization (分工/專門化)
5b. Economies of scale (規模經濟)
6a. 太簡單
6b. Gain 得益
Nominal (5%) = Expected real interest rate (2.7%) + Expected inflation rate (2.3%)
Nominal (5%) = Expected real interest rate (3.3%) + Actual inflation rate (1.7%)
6c. real wages rate(實質工資率)上升
7a. Required reserve (法定儲備) = 1000 million x 20% = 200 million
Excess reserve(超額儲備) = 300 - 200 = 100 million
b.
Original money supply (原來的貨幣供應) = 1000 + 200 = 1200 million
Max. money supply (最大的貨幣供應) = 500/0.2 = 2500 million
Max. possible change in money supply = 2500 - 1200 = 1300 million

8a. Absolute advantage (絕對優勢): Country B (乙國)
8b. Comparative advantage (比較優勢): Country B (乙國)
Opp. cost of producing cars:
Country A: 1T
Country B: 0.6T
8b. Terms of trade: 1C = 0.8T
Total gain from trade = (1 - 0.8) x 10 = 2 units of toy
q1b 下降？ 用小左raw materials

replies.msg: agreed, except q2b, I argued that the subsidy waa assigned according to the region of the candidates but not the income of the candidates, ppl from low income families in other regions received ntg while those from high income group in those regions wud still receive the amount, so it doesnt equalize income
